title: hordeum vulgare subsp. polystichon
intro: Hordeum vulgare subsp. polystichon, also known as six-row hulled barley, is a subspecies of the barley plant that is characterized by its six rows of kernels and a hull that can be easily removed. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and cardiovascular disease. The health benefits of Hordeum vulgare subsp. polystichon include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve insulin sensitivity. Hordeum vulgare subsp. polystichon can be prepared as a tea, extract, or supplement, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
